content.problemStatement:
After the grand Christmas sale, Chef carefully counted his daily earnings. However, he realized that he consistently miscounted **exactly 10 notes every day**.

Given the number of days xxx, determine the **total number of notes Chef missed altogether** over those days.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecountMissedNotescountMissedNotescountMissedNotes – This function calculates the total number of notes Chef miscounted over a given number of days.

Parameters
xxx : An integer representing the number of days Chef counted his earnings.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the total number of notes missed by Chef.
Constraints
1≤x≤101 \leq x \leq 101≤x≤10

Input Format

The first and only line contains a single integer xxx, the number of days.


Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer — the total number of notes Chef missed.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer x (1 ≤ x ≤ 10) — the number of days Chef counted his earnings for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the total number of notes Chef missed, i.e., 10 × x.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are three test cases. For x=1 Chef misses 10*1=10 notes. For x=10 Chef misses 10*10=100 notes. For x=5 Chef misses 10*5=50 notes.
Single test case with x=7, total missed notes = 10*7 = 70.

solution.approach: For each test case we simply multiply the given number of days x by the constant 10, because Chef misses exactly 10 notes per day. The algorithm iterates over all T test cases, performs the multiplication, and prints the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time multiplication per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
